frontier: Smallest N = sum of 3 prime cubes in exactly 6 ways (OEIS A385316 a(6)); known terms a(1..5) = 24, 185527, 8627527, 999979163, 10588881419; published bound a(6) > 499243435237 (~4.99e11).
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Copied the prior-session verifier artifact (prime_cubes_a385316.py from res_20260718_181039), ran default kill-fixture mode and --reproduce mode, and recorded the SHA-256 digest. The verifier builds the complete prime set P={p : p³≤C}, enumerates all nondecreasing triples (p≤q≤r) with p³+q³+r³≤C, counts exact multiplicities, and includes domain kill fixtures (99, 36, 1035 — non-prime components) and predicate kill fixtures (24, 185527 — wrong multiplicity).
verified:
Domain kill fixtures: 3/3 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 2/2 rejected correctly
ALL KILL FIXTURES PASSED
ALL 5 KNOWN TERMS VERIFIED
Artifact: prime_cubes_a385316.py, SHA-256: 3b1071bd04896ae40fd622750afc3d2bef86fb43fa1e9c5b0ee270fee3d27877
Verifier domain: Non-negative integer N is in the domain iff there exist primes p <= q <= r such that N = p^3 + q^3 + r^3 and count(N) = K for K >= 1.
Verifier predicate: A claimed value N* with multiplicity K* is valid iff (1) count({(p,q,r) : p,q,r prime, p<=q<=r, p^3+q^3+r^3=N*}) == K*, and (2) for all M < N*, count(M) != K*.
result: The verifier artifact from the prior session (res_20260718_181039) is confirmed functional and ready for DGX dispatch. Both kill-fixture modes and reproduce mode exit zero with correct output. The bounded search to C=1e12 (dispatch to spark-38e3.local with --cutoff 1000000000000 --mode search) remains the next operational step.
next_gate: Dispatch prime_cubes_a385316.py to DGX spark-38e3.local with `--cutoff 1000000000000 --mode search` for parallel execution; on success, record whether a(6) was found or certified a(6) > 1e12.
